tweets.title: Operation Amistad: Indian Army Field Hospital in Venezuela A local resident details her experience receiving medical care at the Indian Army's field hospital in Caracas, established following the recent 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes. Indian Army Relief Efforts: 1️⃣Rapid Deployment: A medical contingent from the 60 Para Field Hospital was airlifted to Venezuela to provide immediate emergency and trauma care. 2️⃣ Advanced Facilities: The deployment utilizes BHISHM Cubes, rapidly deployable mobile hospitals designed for critical disaster response. 3️⃣Round-the-Clock Operations: Experienced Indian Army medical officers are providing comprehensive care 24/7. 4️⃣ Free Medical Assistance: All consultations, treatments, and prescribed medications are provided entirely free of charge to those impacted by the disaster. The ongoing deployment highlights India's commitment to global humanitarian assistance and disaster relief. @adgpi @SpokespersonMoD @HQ_IDS_India @DefenceMinIndia @rajnathsingh @MEAIndia @drs
tweets.content:
Operation Amistad: Indian Army Field Hospital in Venezuela

A local resident details her experience receiving medical care at the Indian Army's field hospital in Caracas, established following the recent 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes.

Indian Army Relief Efforts:

1️⃣Rapid Deployment: A medical contingent from the 60 Para Field Hospital was airlifted to Venezuela to provide immediate emergency and trauma care.

2️⃣ Advanced Facilities: The deployment utilizes BHISHM Cubes, rapidly deployable mobile hospitals designed for critical disaster response.

3️⃣Round-the-Clock Operations: Experienced Indian Army medical officers are providing comprehensive care 24/7.

4️⃣ Free Medical Assistance: All consultations, treatments, and prescribed medications are provided entirely free of charge to those impacted by the disaster.

The ongoing deployment highlights India's commitment to global humanitarian assistance and disaster relief.

@adgpi @SpokespersonMoD @HQ_IDS_India @DefenceMinIndia @rajnathsingh @MEAIndia @drs

tweets.title: BREAKING: Pakistan Opposition Leaders Blocked from Entering Pakistan Occupied Kashmir (PoK) The Jammu Kashmir Joint Awami Action Committee (JKJAAC) has accused Pakistani authorities of suppressing democratic rights after a delegation of opposition leaders was reportedly stopped from entering Pakistan-occupied Jammu & Kashmir (PoJK). According to JKJAAC, the delegation, led by Mahmood Khan Achakzai and including former Prime Minister Shahid Khaqan Abbasi and other opposition figures, was halted at Kotli and prevented from proceeding further. The committee also alleged restrictions on movement and supplies, describing them as part of a broader crackdown on dissent. JKJAAC reiterated that its movement is peaceful and vowed to continue protests until its demands are addressed.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: Pakistan Opposition Leaders Blocked from Entering Pakistan Occupied Kashmir (PoK)

The Jammu Kashmir Joint Awami Action Committee (JKJAAC) has accused Pakistani authorities of suppressing democratic rights after a delegation of opposition leaders was reportedly stopped from entering Pakistan-occupied Jammu & Kashmir (PoJK).

According to JKJAAC, the delegation, led by Mahmood Khan Achakzai and including former Prime Minister Shahid Khaqan Abbasi and other opposition figures, was halted at Kotli and prevented from proceeding further.

The committee also alleged restrictions on movement and supplies, describing them as part of a broader crackdown on dissent. JKJAAC reiterated that its movement is peaceful and vowed to continue protests until its demands are addressed.

tweets.title: General Upendra Dwivedi concludes his tenure as the 30th Chief of Army Staff, handing over command to General Dhiraj Seth. His tenure marked a distinct operational shift for the Indian Army, transitioning it toward a highly modernized, outcome-driven force. Key highlights from his leadership: - The Five Pillars: Gen Dwivedi executed a transformation framework focused on technology absorption, agile structural reorganization, and advanced human resource upskilling. - Tech & Self-Reliance: Championed indigenous defense manufacturing, overseeing the integration of roughly 50,000 drones alongside AI, big data, and quantum tech into daily operations. - Combat Readiness: Validated new doctrines and maintained strict vigilance through major deployments, notably Operation Snow Leopard on the northern borders and Operation Sindoor on the western front. - Tri-Service Synergy: Accelerated joint interoperability among the Army, Navy, and Air Force, laying critical groundwork for theaterization. - Specialized Units: Raised specialized combat formations explicitly trained to handle multi-domain threats and mass drone warfare.
tweets.content:
General Upendra Dwivedi concludes his tenure as the 30th Chief of Army Staff, handing over command to General Dhiraj Seth. His tenure marked a distinct operational shift for the Indian Army, transitioning it toward a highly modernized, outcome-driven force.

Key highlights from his leadership:

- The Five Pillars: Gen Dwivedi executed a transformation framework focused on technology absorption, agile structural reorganization, and advanced human resource upskilling.

- Tech & Self-Reliance: Championed indigenous defense manufacturing, overseeing the integration of roughly 50,000 drones alongside AI, big data, and quantum tech into daily operations.

- Combat Readiness: Validated new doctrines and maintained strict vigilance through major deployments, notably Operation Snow Leopard on the northern borders and Operation Sindoor on the western front.

- Tri-Service Synergy: Accelerated joint interoperability among the Army, Navy, and Air Force, laying critical groundwork for theaterization.

- Specialized Units: Raised specialized combat formations explicitly trained to handle multi-domain threats and mass drone warfare.

The Nah Welfare Society (NWS) in Arunachal Pradesh has alleged that the PLA has gradually occupied parts of its traditional grazing, hunting, and agricultural land in Upper Subansiri district over the past six years.

Differences may arise. Frustration may surface. That happens even between blood brothers. But public mudslinging only hands our adversaries exactly what they want, a divided security establishment.

I appeal to serving personnel of JKP and Army, veterans, the media, and social media users: stop turning this into “JKP vs Army.” Do not amplify the divide. Do not fuel emotions. Let responsible voices prevail.

Our enemy is focused on attacking India. We should remain focused on defeating the enemy, not each other.

Enough is enough.

LET IT STOP NOW.

tweets.title: China Demonstrates High-Manoeuvrability Tailless Sixth-Generation Fighter New footage reportedly shows China's tailless three-engine sixth-generation fighter performing a sharp turn-climb, demonstrating a level of manoeuvrability long considered difficult for tailless aircraft. Developed by Chengdu Aircraft Corporation, the aircraft has been in flight testing since late 2024, with four prototypes built. The design relies on advanced flight-control software and wing-mounted control surfaces instead of conventional horizontal tailplanes. If validated, the demonstration marks a milestone in overcoming one of the key technical challenges of sixth-generation fighter development while retaining the stealth advantages of a tailless design. China is currently the only country known to have multiple tailless sixth-generation fighter prototypes in flight testing.

tweets.title: India Conducts First Jet Landing Using GAGAN India has successfully conducted its first satellite-based landing system approach on a jet aircraft, with an IndiGo Airbus A320 performing an LPV (Localiser Performance with Vertical Guidance) approach at Udaipur Airport using the indigenous GAGAN navigation system. Developed jointly by ISRO and the Airports Authority of India (AAI), GAGAN provides satellite-based precision guidance, reducing reliance on ground-based landing infrastructure. This marks the first use of GAGAN for jet operations in India, extending satellite-based precision approaches beyond turboprop aircraft and strengthening India's indigenous aviation navigation capabilities.
tweets.content:

India has successfully conducted its first satellite-based landing system approach on a jet aircraft, with an IndiGo Airbus A320 performing an LPV (Localiser Performance with Vertical Guidance) approach at Udaipur Airport using the indigenous GAGAN navigation system.

Developed jointly by ISRO and the Airports Authority of India (AAI), GAGAN provides satellite-based precision guidance, reducing reliance on ground-based landing infrastructure.

This marks the first use of GAGAN for jet operations in India, extending satellite-based precision approaches beyond turboprop aircraft and strengthening India's indigenous aviation navigation capabilities.

📍 Son of Mohammad Yaqoob Khan, resident of Taroti village, Tetrinote tehsil, Haveli district, PoK
📍 Crossed into Indian side of LoC before being detained by security forces
📍 Initial search report confirms no incriminating material recovered

📍 Security agencies examining possible routes used for crossing

📍 Earlier two Pakistani nationals were also apprehended in separate LoC incidents during the same period
📍 Both earlier cases are under investigation by security agencies
📍 Heightened surveillance and alert maintained along Balakote stretch of LoC

The claim comes after U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) said it conducted fresh strikes on Iranian military infrastructure following alleged Iranian drone attacks on commercial vessels near the Strait of Hormuz. tweets.content:
BREAKING: IRGC Claims Strikes on U.S. Military Sites in Kuwait & Bahrain

Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claims it launched a joint ballistic missile and drone operation targeting the Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait and the U.S. Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain, saying it destroyed eight U.S. military facilities in retaliation for recent American strikes on Iranian targets.

The claim comes after U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) said it conducted fresh strikes on Iranian military infrastructure following alleged Iranian drone attacks on commercial vessels near the Strait of Hormuz.

Meanwhile, Kuwait said its air defenses intercepted hostile missiles and drones, while Bahrain activated warning sirens and urged residents to seek shelter.

tweets.title: 🚨 Pakistan's Cross Border Drone Drug Smuggling Bid Exposed A drone carrying 15 kg of narcotics crashed before it could enter India, exposing a cross border smuggling attempt from Pakistan. The drone crashed down in a residential area, scattering sealed packets that were later recovered by pak police after locals complaint. The incident occurred in Narang Mandi, Sheikhupura district, Pakistan's Punjab province, located close to the international border opposite India's Punjab, highlighting yet another route used for attempted drone based narcotics smuggling. A drone carrying 15 kg of narcotics crashed before it could enter India, exposing a cross border smuggling attempt from Pakistan.

The incident occurred in Narang Mandi, Sheikhupura district, Pakistan's Punjab province, located close to the international border opposite India's Punjab, highlighting yet another route used for attempted drone based narcotics smuggling.
